title: Iterators, Generators, and Lazy Pipelines · Changed-Input Lab
summary: Stream large activity exports through bounded-memory validation and aggregation stages. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a lazy activity-processing pipeline with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish iterable, iterator, iter, next, StopIteration, restartability, and one-pass consumption.
Write generator functions with yield, local suspended state, return behavior, and cleanup-aware consumption.
Use generator expressions for single-pass pipelines while materializing when reuse or random access is required.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
